title: सौन्दर्यलहरी (चयन)
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सौन्दर्यलहरी (चयन) ॥

शिवः शक्त्या युक्तो यदि भवति शक्तः प्रभवितुं
न चेदेवं देवो न खलु कुशलः स्पन्दितुमपि।
अतस्त्वामाराध्यां हरिहरविरिञ्चादिभिरपि
प्रणन्तुं स्तोतुं वा कथमकृतपुण्यः प्रभवति॥ १॥

तनीयांसं पांसुं तव चरणपंकेरुहभवं
विरिञ्चिस्सन्धत्ते विरचयितुं लोकानविकलम्।
वहत्येनं शौरिः कथमपि सहस्रेण शिरसां
हरस्संक्षुद्यैनं भजति भसितोद्धूलनविधिम्॥ २॥

अविद्यानामन्तस्तिमिरमिहिरद्वीपनगरी
जडानां चैतन्यस्तबकमकरन्दश्रुतिझरी।
दरिद्राणां चिन्तामणिगुणनिका जन्मजलधौ
निमग्नानां दंष्ट्रा मुररिपुवराहस्य भवति॥ ३॥

त्वदन्यः पाणिभ्यामभयवरदो दैवतगणः
त्वमेका नैवासि प्रकटितवराभीत्यभिनया।
भयात् त्रातुं दातुं फलमपि च वाञ्छासमधिकं
शरण्ये लोकानां तव हि चरणावेव निपुणौ॥ ४॥

हरिस्त्वामाराध्य प्रणतजनसौभाग्यजननीम्
पुरा नारी भूत्वा पुररिपुमपि क्षोभमनयत्।
स्मरोऽपि त्वां नत्वा रतिनयनलेह्येन वपुषा
मुनीनामप्यन्तः प्रभवति हि मोहाय महताम्॥ ५॥

॥ इति श्री सौन्दर्यलहरी (चयन) सम्पूर्णम् ॥

Invokes blessings of Lalita in all forms
Traditionally recited for removal of sins and bestows spiritual merit
Ideal for Lalita festivals and daily worship
description: Soundarya Lahari Excerpt (सौन्दर्यलहरी (चयन)) is a sacred prayer dedicated to Goddess Parvati. This sacred composition is sourced from Adi Shankaracharya and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to remove sins and bestow spiritual merit and invoke blessings of Lalita in all forms. It is also said to grant divine protection and auspiciousness. It is especially recommended on Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri and Lalita Panchami for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 5 verses and takes around 4 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Soundarya Lahari Excerpt in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Soundarya Lahari Excerpt?
seoFaqs.answer: The Soundarya Lahari Excerpt (सौन्दर्यलहरी चयन) is a curated selection of the most-celebrated verses from the Soundarya Lahari (सौन्दर्यलहरी — Wave of Beauty) — the supreme 100-verse Sanskrit hymn composed by Adi Shankaracharya in praise of Goddess Lalita Maha-Tripurasundari (Devi/Parvati in Her supreme Sri Vidya form). The full Soundarya Lahari has two parts: verses 1-41 called Ananda Lahari (Wave of Bliss — focusing on philosophical-mystical aspects of Devi's transcendent reality) and verses 42-100 called Soundarya Lahari (focusing on Devi's iconographic-physical beauty from head-crown to toe-anklets). This excerpt presents 5 key verses including the famous opening Shivah shaktya yukto yadi bhavati shaktah prabhavitum — Shiva united with Shakti can act, otherwise He cannot even stir. The text is preserved in classical Sri Vidya tradition. (Source: Adi Shankaracharya — Soundarya Lahari verses 1-5.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the famous opening verse Shivah Shaktya Yukto mean?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening verse — Shivah shaktya yukto yadi bhavati shaktah prabhavitum, Na chedevam devo na khalu kushalah spanditum-api; Atas-tvam-aradhyam Hari-Hara-Virinchadibhir-api, Pranantum stotum va kathamakrita-punyah prabhavati — is one of the most theologically profound verses in all of Hindu literature. Translation: If Shiva is united with Shakti, then He becomes capable of creating; otherwise the Lord cannot even stir; therefore, You who are worshipped even by Hari (Vishnu), Hara (Shiva) and Virinchi (Brahma) — how can one without accumulated merit (akrita-punyah) ever bow to You or praise You? Theologically this establishes: (1) Shakti is the active cosmic-power without which even Brahman cannot act; (2) Devi is supreme — worshipped by the Trinity itself; (3) approaching the Divine Mother requires past-life spiritual-merit (punya). This verse alone has spawned dozens of commentaries by Lakshmidhara, Bhaskararaya, Kameshwara Surya and others. (Sources: Soundarya Lahari verse 1; Bhaskararaya Saubhagya-Bhaskara.)

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Soundarya Lahari and is it really by Adi Shankaracharya?
seoFaqs.answer: The Soundarya Lahari is traditionally attributed to Adi Shankaracharya (788-820 CE) — the founder of Advaita Vedanta. Per the legendary account, Shankaracharya visited Mount Kailasha and Lord Shiva himself gave him a divine palm-leaf manuscript of the entire Soundarya Lahari. As Shankaracharya was descending Kailasha, Lord Nandi (Shiva's bull) snatched away the first 41 verses (Ananda Lahari portion) leaving only verses 42-100 with him. Shankaracharya then composed the first 41 verses himself from memory. Some modern scholars debate whether the Ananda Lahari portion (heavily Tantric in content) is truly by the Advaitin Shankaracharya or by a later Sri Vidya master, since Shankaracharya is more associated with nirguna-Brahman than with Tantric Devi-worship. However, the orthodox Sringeri-Kanchi traditions firmly attribute the entire 100-verse Soundarya Lahari to Adi Shankaracharya — and he is regarded as both the supreme Advaitin AND the supreme Sri Vidya master who installed Sri Chakra at Kanchi-Kamakshi, Sringeri-Sharadamba, Tirupati-Padmavati. (Sources: Sringeri tradition; Madhaviya Shankara Digvijaya.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Ananda Lahari and Soundarya Lahari?
seoFaqs.answer: The full 100-verse text is structured in two distinct parts: (1) Ananda Lahari (Wave of Bliss) — verses 1-41 — focuses on the transcendent-mystical aspects of Devi: Her identity with Brahman, Her relationship with Shiva, the Sri Chakra yantra, the Kundalini ascent through the chakras (Muladhara to Sahasrara), the cosmic creation through Devi's will, the Panchadashi-Shodashi mantras, and esoteric Sri-Vidya philosophy. Each verse of Ananda Lahari is itself a Tantric mantra with specific yantra, beeja, and phala — making it a complete sadhana text. (2) Soundarya Lahari (Wave of Beauty) — verses 42-100 — provides the most-detailed kesha-adi-pada-anta (head-to-toe) iconographic description of Devi: Her hair, crown, forehead, eyes, nose, lips, teeth, smile, ears, neck, breasts, waist, hips, thighs, knees, calves, ankles, feet — each described with extraordinary poetic-imagery and metaphysical-significance. Together they form the complete sadhana text. (Sources: Lakshmidhara commentary; Sringeri Sri Vidya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should Soundarya Lahari Excerpt be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Soundarya Lahari Excerpt is most powerfully recited on Friday (Shukravar — Devi's day), Tuesday (Mangalvar — Shakti day), every Pournami (full moon), all 9 nights of Sharad Navratri (especially Maha-Ashtami), Chaitra Navratri Mahanavami, Lalita Jayanti (Magha Shukla Panchami), Lalita Panchami (Ashvin Shukla Panchami), Shankara Jayanti (Vaishakha Shukla Panchami — birthday of Adi Shankaracharya), and during Sri Chakra Navavarana puja. The proper procedure: (1) bathe and wear red or yellow clothes; (2) light a deepa before a Sri Chakra yantra or Devi image; (3) take sankalpa with vinayoga (specifying Devata as Lalita Tripurasundari, Bija as Aim Hrim Shrim, Shakti as Lalita); (4) recite the verses with focus on each verse's specific yantra and beeja-mantra; (5) offer red-flowers (japa-pushpa or kanavera), kumkum, fruits; (6) ideal counts are 9, 11, 21, or 108 times; (7) advanced sadhakas use each verse for a specific siddhi via 40-day prayoga as detailed in commentaries. (Sources: Lakshmidhara prayoga manual; Sringeri Sri Vidya paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Soundarya Lahari Excerpt and full Soundarya Lahari?
seoFaqs.answer: The full Soundarya Lahari is a 100-verse magnum-opus that takes 60-90 minutes to recite — making it a serious commitment for daily sadhana. The Soundarya Lahari Excerpt presents 5 key verses (the opening verses 1-5 of Ananda Lahari) which can be recited in 4-5 minutes — making it accessible for: (1) busy householders who cannot recite the full 100 verses daily but want the essential spiritual-benefits; (2) beginners new to Sri Vidya who are starting their sadhana; (3) those without Sri Vidya diksha — since the full text contains advanced Tantric content requiring initiation; (4) daily archana-puja where the excerpt is chanted as part of larger Devi-worship; (5) introduction to the Soundarya Lahari before deeper study. The selected verses are: verse 1 (Shivah shaktya yukto), verse 2 (Taniyamsam pamsum tava charanapankeruhabhavam), verse 3 (Avidyanam antas-timira-mihira-dvipa-nagari), verse 4 (Tvad-anyah panibhyam abhaya-varado daivata-ganah), and verse 5 (Haris-tvam-aradhya pranata-jana-saubhagya-jananim) — covering the Devi-supremacy, foot-worship, Devi-as-light, Devi-as-protectress, and Vishnu-Shiva worshipping Devi themes. The excerpt is ideal for daily sadhana while one progresses to the full text. (Sources: Sri Vidya parampara teaching guidelines.)
